package handler

import (
	"context"
	"database/sql"
)

// Positive scenario for package_name_role_drift.
// Rule intent: Packages whose names advertise one role (e.g., handler) but whose
// exported symbols primarily belong to another role (e.g., repository).

// This package is named "handler" but contains repository/data-access logic,
// which is the anti-pattern. The package name misleads about its actual role.

type UserHandler struct {
	db *sql.DB
}

// These methods are repository-style data access, not handler logic.
func (h *UserHandler) FindByID(ctx context.Context, id string) (*User, error) {
	row := h.db.QueryContext(ctx, "SELECT id, name, email FROM users WHERE id = $1", id)
	var u User
	err := row.Scan(&u.ID, &u.Name, &u.Email)
	return &u, err
}

func (h *UserHandler) FindAll(ctx context.Context) ([]User, error) {
	rows, err := h.db.QueryContext(ctx, "SELECT id, name, email FROM users")
	if err != nil {
		return nil, err
	}
	defer rows.Close()

	var users []User
	for rows.Next() {
		var u User
		rows.Scan(&u.ID, &u.Name, &u.Email)
		users = append(users, u)
	}
	return users, nil
}

func (h *UserHandler) Create(ctx context.Context, user *User) error {
	_, err := h.db.ExecContext(ctx,
		"INSERT INTO users (id, name, email) VALUES ($1, $2, $3)",
		user.ID, user.Name, user.Email)
	return err
}

func (h *UserHandler) Delete(ctx context.Context, id string) error {
	_, err := h.db.ExecContext(ctx, "DELETE FROM users WHERE id = $1", id)
	return err
}

func (h *UserHandler) UpdateEmail(ctx context.Context, id, email string) error {
	_, err := h.db.ExecContext(ctx, "UPDATE users SET email = $1 WHERE id = $2", email, id)
	return err
}

type User struct {
	ID    string
	Name  string
	Email string
}
